steps.title: Simplify the inequality
steps.content:
Starting with $ax - 21 \le 14$, add 21 to both sides:
$$ax \le 35$$

steps.title: Recognize why a must be negative
steps.content: The solution $x \ge -7$ is a lower bound. When dividing an inequality by a value, the direction reverses only if that value is negative. Since $ax \le 35$ yields $x \ge -7$ (a reversal), we must have $a \lt 0$.

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content:
Divide both sides of $ax \le 35$ by the negative value $a$:
$$x \ge \frac{35}{a}$$
Matching this to the solution $x \ge -7$:
$$\frac{35}{a} = -7$$
Solving: $35 = -7a$, which gives $a = -5$. The greatest possible value of $a$ is -5.
